description: Milestone 3a: Real OAuth2/OpenID Connect authentication & session management using AppAuth-iOS (Authorization Code + PKCE). Establish secure token storage, refresh, logout, and authenticated HTTP client plumbing. No Discourse API integration yet.

userStories.title: Add AppAuth-iOS dependency and basic OIDC discovery wiring
userStories.description: Integrate AppAuth-iOS via SPM and implement OIDC discovery against the issuer. Do not hardcode endpoints; use discovery.
acceptanceCriteria: AppAuth-iOS is added via Swift Package Manager and builds
OIDC discovery loads configuration from issuer https://sso.piratenpartei.de/realms/Piratenlogin
No endpoints are hardcoded; authorization/token endpoints come from discovery
Discovery errors are surfaced as user-friendly auth errors
Context7 consulted for AppAuth-iOS integration and OIDC discovery usage; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Implement login flow with ASWebAuthenticationSession via AppAuth (PKCE)
userStories.description: Replace fake login toggle with real login using AppAuth authorization code + PKCE and the custom redirect URI.
acceptanceCriteria: Login initiates AppAuth authorization request with code_challenge (PKCE) and scopes: openid profile offline_access
Redirect URI de.meine-piraten://oauth-callback is handled in-app (Info.plist configured)
On success, app transitions to authenticated state and displays the main tabs
On failure/cancel, app returns to unauthenticated state with a non-crashing error message
Context7 consulted for iOS URL scheme redirect handling + AppAuth best practices; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Persist tokens securely and restore session on app launch
userStories.description: Store refresh/access/id tokens in Keychain via CredentialStore and restore authenticated session on relaunch if valid.
acceptanceCriteria: Tokens are stored using KeychainCredentialStore (no plaintext persistence)
On app launch, session restoration checks token presence and validity (expiration if available) and restores state
No token values are logged
Unit tests cover storing and retrieving token bundle (can use test keychain entries)
Context7 consulted for secure token storage patterns on iOS; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Implement token refresh and expiration handling
userStories.description: Use AppAuth token refresh with refresh_token and handle expiry/refresh failures gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: A TokenRefresher component exists (or equivalent) that refreshes access tokens using AppAuth
Authenticated API calls (stubbed) can request a valid access token and trigger refresh if needed
If refresh fails (revoked/expired), user is transitioned to unauthenticated with a clear message
Context7 consulted for AppAuth token refresh patterns and error handling; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Create AuthenticatedHTTPClient boundary (no real endpoints yet)
userStories.description: Introduce an HTTP client abstraction that can attach Bearer tokens and centralize error mapping for future API clients.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTPClient protocol exists with request building and response decoding helpers
AuthenticatedHTTPClient attaches Authorization: Bearer <access_token> when authenticated
401/403 responses map to a domain auth error and can trigger re-auth flow (no infinite loops)
No Discourse endpoints are implemented yet; include a small local stub request example if needed
Context7 consulted for URLSession modern patterns (async/await) and auth header best practices; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Implement logout and session cleanup
userStories.description: Provide a robust logout that clears tokens and resets app state. (Optional: end-session endpoint if present via discovery.)
acceptanceCriteria: Logout clears token bundle from KeychainCredentialStore
Auth state resets to unauthenticated and UI returns to login screen
If discovery provides an end_session_endpoint, document decision to use or not use it in Docs/DECISIONS.md
Context7 consulted for OIDC logout/end-session guidance and AppAuth considerations; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Document auth configuration and open questions
userStories.description: Capture the exact auth config and any remaining unknowns for Discourse session propagation and messaging auth.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs/DECISIONS.md includes: issuer, clientID, redirectUri, scopes, and why AppAuth-iOS was chosen
Docs/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md includes: how Discourse accepts auth (bearer vs cookie vs separate SSO), required scopes/claims, token lifetimes/rotation policies
No secrets are committed; only public client configuration is documented
Context7 consulted for OIDC discovery fields and recommended native app flow (RFC 8252); summary added to progress.txt
